mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in non-farm payrolls reduces labor market tightness, lowering wage-push inflation expectations and compressing equity risk premiums, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital structurally elevates, discounting future corporate earnings more heavily and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment and elevated inflation creates stagflationary pressure, forcing the Federal Reserve to prioritize growth stabilization over price stability, which historically compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample Sharpe ratios.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing construction, consumer wealth effects and construction-related economic activity boost corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as market participants price in near-term demand expansion.
failure_condition: HOUST growth decouples from corporate earnings growth, resulting in a negative correlation between housing starts and 5-day forward SPX returns across two consecutive economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP signals a demand shock that reduces corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings revisions cascade through equity markets.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in GDP contractions within 5 trading days, rendering the 60-day forward return signal statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces consumer credit growth, supply chain bottlenecks ease while demand softens, creating a deflationary supply glut that pressures corporate pricing power and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and RSAFS growth rates shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns across all market regimes.
failure_condition: The historical negative correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and SPX forward returns breaks down during periods of quantitative tightening, resulting in a negative sign of the predictive coefficient over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment environment, subsequent monthly job growth acts as a leading indicator of economic stabilization, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as recession fears dissipate and risk appetite rebounds.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to reverse equity drawdowns when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, with 20-day forward returns remaining negative in > 55% of triggered instances over two consecutive recessions.
